scenario: Cobalt Banking computes a service fee inside a Camunda 8.8 payments form for 950 transactions per day using an expression field bound to the key fee. The Target value holds a FEEL expression that aggregates two other form inputs, and the team needs the computed value to refresh live every time any contributing input changes rather than waiting for the operator to submit.
question: Which Compute on configuration must the team pick on the expression field to satisfy the live recalculation requirement?

options.text: The option that evaluates the Target value expression whenever the result changes, so the computed field updates live as the contributing inputs change
The option that evaluates the Target value expression only on form submission, so the computed field updates a single time at the point of submit
The option that evaluates the Deactivate if expression on every keystroke, so the computed field updates after each deactivation check is run
The option that evaluates the Target value expression on form load only, so the computed field carries the initial value through to the submission step

a.text: Correct. The Compute on property documents two values: whenever the result changes or only on form submission. The first matches the requirement to refresh the computed field as contributing inputs change.

b.text: Incorrect. Evaluating only on form submission means the operator sees a stale value during entry, which contradicts the live recalculation requirement described in the scenario.

c.text: Incorrect. Deactivate if is a separate property that gates whether the expression runs at all; it is not a Compute on option and does not drive the evaluation cadence.

d.text: Incorrect. The Compute on property does not offer a form-load-only choice; the documented values are result-change and form-submission, so this configuration is not selectable.

explanation: The expression field exposes a Compute on property with two documented values: evaluate the Target value whenever the result changes, or evaluate only on form submission. The live-update requirement maps to the result-change option.
